title: Creamy Smoky Potato Stir-fry
description: A quick and easy weeknight dish featuring creamy potatoes cooked in a smoky vegetable shortening sauce, perfect for those looking to reduce their fat intake while maintaining the authentic Indian (Punjabi) flavors.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.amount: enough to make a paste with sugar and cayenne pepper

instructions: Boil the potatoes in water until tender. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix the water, sugar, cayenne pepper, and salt to form a smooth paste.
Heat vegetable shortening in a pan over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until translucent, then add garlic and cook for another minute.
Stir in cumin seeds, coriander powder, curry leaves, and the sugar-cayenne pepper water mixture. Simmer for 2-3 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Add boiled potatoes to the pan with a little bit of the cooking liquid from the potatoes, tossing them around so they are evenly coated with sauce.
Simmer for another minute until the potatoes absorb the sauce and are tender.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice.
